text: After months of careful planning, I finally set off on my trip to Japan. Everything was new to me—the customs, the way people greeted each other, and especially the food. I tried sushi from a small street stall and was surprised by how fresh it tasted. Language barriers were tricky at times, but smiling and pointing usually worked. I learned a few basic phrases like "thank you" and "how much," which helped a lot. Despite the challenges, every moment felt special. Looking back, the memories of those warm evenings and friendly strangers are what I treasure most. Travel truly opens your heart and mind.
